title: helianthus carnosus
intro: Helianthus carnosus, also known as the Hairy Sunflower, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Helianthus carnosus include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and pneumonia. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by drying the leaves and flowers, then steeping them in hot water or mixing with a solvent to create a medicinal extract.
